rawText:
Right now, robots are criss-crossing the surface of Mars, providing us with a unique insight into one of our closest neighbours in the Solar System.

It is an incredible feat of science and engineering -- but it seems we are not done yet. Could humans actually reach and live on Mars?

Compared to the Moon, the challenges of setting foot on Mars are astronomical -- and yet nations and companies around the world are already working to make crewed missions to Mars a reality.

The scope and risk involved in a crewed Earth-Mars mission far exceeds that of a trip to the Moon and back. A voyage to the Moon takes approximately three days with current technology, while crews would face two to three years without seeing other humans besides crewmates during a Mars mission.

The crew must endure no natural day and night cycle, a reduction in tasting ability, muscle wastage and bone-density loss over time.

Beyond Earth's atmosphere, astronauts on the International Space Station are exposed to 250 times the radiation experienced on Earth's surface. This increases to 700 times the radiation for missions beyond Earth's orbit, without the protection of Earth's magnetic field. Mars missions would combine long mission length with high radiation exposure.

NASA and the ESA conducted studies following twins Scott and Mark Kelly. Scott conducted lengthy ISS missions while Mark remained on Earth. Results showed Scott's immune system reacted to vaccines the same way as Mark's, meaning vaccines administered during long space missions should remain effective.

The research revealed differences in Scott's gene expression and changes in telomeres. Telomeres are the ends of DNA strands that protect chromosomes from damage. Scott's telomeres lengthened in space compared to Mark's. While long telomeres were once thought to assist with longevity, extreme lengths increase cancer risk and other disorders.

To examine psychological challenges, researchers have conducted simulated Mars missions with analogue astronauts -- people role-playing astronauts without actually going to space.

Mars 500 was one prominent study conducted by the Russian Academy of Sciences' Institute of Biomedical Problems as a collaborative effort between Russian, European and Chinese space agencies. Over 17 months, six analogue astronauts lived in a simulated Mars mission environment.

By including various nationalities, cultures and personalities, researchers could study team cohesion, conflict resolution, leadership, and problem-solving. Results were generally very positive, with little friction from cultural differences and natural inclinations toward creativity and celebration of milestones.

However, maintaining synchronized sleeping patterns proved problematic. With only artificial light and dark modes -- no natural sunlight -- some crew experienced disrupted sleeping patterns leading to poorer test performance. The entire crew generally slept more as the mission progressed, especially during the return phase.

The first crewed Mars mission likely will not land on the surface initially. One concept involves building an orbital space station first, reducing risks for crews getting onto and off the planet. Astronauts aboard a Mars space station could use remote-controlled robots to scout potential habitat locations.

Once advance supplies and tools reach a safe landing site, survival challenges begin.

Basic requirements include breathable air, food, water, fuel and shelter. Without regular shipments from Earth, Mars settlers must extract needed resources from the local environment -- a process called "in-situ resource utilisation."

overview.title: How Could Humans Live on Mars?

sentences.original: Right now, robots are criss-crossing the surface of Mars, providing us with a unique insight into one of our closest neighbours in the Solar System.
sentences.translation: 바로 지금, 로봇들이 화성의 표면을 종횡무진 누비며, 태양계에서 가장 가까운 이웃 중 하나에 대한 독특한 통찰을 우리에게 제공하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: providing us with a unique insight into one of our closest neighbours in the Solar System
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과/동시 동작)

vocabulary.example: Roads criss-cross the countryside.
The study provides insight into human behavior.
Mars is one of Earth's closest neighbours.
The surface of the Moon is covered with craters.

expressions.usage: 현재 진행 중인 상황을 강조할 때
누군가에게 무언가를 주거나 제공할 때

patterns.explanation: 현재진행형으로 동작을 묘사하고, 분사구문으로 그 결과를 덧붙이는 패턴
examples: Scientists are studying the ocean, providing us with new data.
Drones are surveying the area, providing rescuers with vital information.

drillQuestions.question: Robots are criss-crossing Mars, _____ us with unique insight.
The research provides us _____ new knowledge.

sentences.notes: 기사의 첫 문장으로, 현재진행형을 사용해 생동감을 준다. neighbours는 영국식 철자(미국식: neighbors)이다. 화성을 "이웃"에 비유한 것은 친근함을 더한다.

koreanAlignment.en: one of our closest neighbours in the Solar System
koreanAlignment.ko: 태양계에서 가장 가까운 이웃 중 하나

sentences.original: It is an incredible feat of science and engineering -- but it seems we are not done yet.
sentences.translation: 이것은 놀라운 과학과 공학의 위업이다 — 하지만 우리는 아직 끝나지 않은 것 같다.

grammarElements.text: an incredible feat of science and engineering
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: but it seems we are not done yet
grammarElements.label: 대조절

vocabulary.example: The view from the mountain was incredible.
Climbing Everest is an amazing feat.

expressions.usage: 더 할 일이 남아있음을 나타낼 때
불확실하거나 조심스러운 판단을 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 긍정적 평가 후 대시로 전환하여 반전이나 추가 관점을 제시하는 패턴
examples: It is a great achievement -- but there is still more work to do.
It is an important step -- but challenges remain.

drillQuestions.question: It is an incredible _____ of science.

sentences.notes: feat(/fiːt/)와 feet(/fiːt/)는 동음이의어이다. 대시(--)를 사용하여 앞의 칭찬에 이어 반전("아직 끝나지 않았다")을 만드는 글쓰기 기법이 효과적이다.

sentences.original: Could humans actually reach and live on Mars?
sentences.translation: 인간이 실제로 화성에 도달하고 살 수 있을까?

vocabulary.example: Did you actually see it happen?
We reached the summit after six hours.

expressions.usage: 가능성에 대한 진지한 의문을 제기할 때

patterns.explanation: 가능성에 대한 의문문. could는 can보다 더 불확실하고 사색적인 느낌을 준다.
examples: Could we actually travel faster than light?
Could technology actually solve climate change?

drillQuestions.question: _____ humans actually live on Mars?

sentences.notes: 수사적 질문(rhetorical question)으로 독자의 호기심을 유발한다. could는 can보다 가능성이 더 낮거나 사색적인 뉘앙스를 전달한다.
pronunciationNotes: actually: /ˈæktʃuəli/ — "액추얼리", 첫 음절에 강세. 한국인은 "엑츄얼리"로 발음하기 쉬운데 /æ/ 소리에 주의
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수사적 질문(rhetorical question) — 기사 전체의 핵심 질문을 던져 독자의 관심을 유도

sentences.original: Compared to the Moon, the challenges of setting foot on Mars are astronomical -- and yet nations and companies around the world are already working to make crewed missions to Mars a reality.
sentences.translation: 달에 비하면, 화성에 발을 딛는 것의 도전은 천문학적이다 — 그럼에도 전 세계의 국가와 기업들은 이미 유인 화성 탐사를 현실로 만들기 위해 노력하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: the challenges of setting foot on Mars
grammarElements.label: 주어 (동명사 포함)

grammarElements.text: and yet nations and companies around the world are already working to make crewed missions to Mars a reality
grammarElements.label: 양보/대조절

vocabulary.example: The cost of the project was astronomical.
The crewed mission launched successfully.
Virtual reality technology is improving fast.

expressions.usage: 어딘가에 처음으로 도달하는 것을 표현할 때
앞의 내용과 대조되는 사실을 강조할 때
꿈이나 계획을 실현하는 것을 나타낼 때

patterns.pattern: Compared to [A], [B] is/are [adjective] -- and yet [contrasting fact]
patterns.explanation: 비교와 대조를 한 문장에 담는 패턴. 비교 후 반전을 제시한다.
examples: Compared to walking, flying is expensive -- and yet millions travel by plane every day.

drillQuestions.question: _____ to the Moon, Mars challenges are astronomical.
Nations are working to make Mars missions a _____.

sentences.notes: astronomical은 이중 의미(pun/wordplay)이다: 1) 천문학의(literal), 2) 어마어마한(figurative). 화성 탐사 기사에서 이 단어를 사용한 것은 의도적인 언어 유희이다.

koreanAlignment.en: the challenges of setting foot on Mars
nations and companies around the world
crewed missions to Mars a reality

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 언어 유희(pun) — astronomical이 "천문학적"과 "어마어마한" 두 가지 의미를 동시에 전달

sentences.original: The scope and risk involved in a crewed Earth-Mars mission far exceeds that of a trip to the Moon and back.
sentences.translation: 유인 지구-화성 임무에 관련된 규모와 위험은 달 왕복 여행의 그것을 훨씬 초과한다.

grammarElements.text: The scope and risk involved in a crewed Earth-Mars mission
grammarElements.label: 주어 (과거분사 수식 포함)

grammarElements.text: that of a trip to the Moon and back
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (that = 대명사)

vocabulary.example: The scope of the project is very large.
The cost exceeded our budget.
The risks involved in space travel are significant.

expressions.usage: 두 가지를 비교할 때 차이가 매우 클 경우
반복을 피하기 위해 앞서 언급한 명사를 가리킬 때

patterns.pattern: The [noun] involved in [A] far exceeds that of [B]
patterns.explanation: A에 수반되는 것이 B의 그것을 훨씬 초과한다는 비교 패턴
examples: The effort involved in a PhD far exceeds that of a master's degree.
The cost involved in building a house far exceeds that of renting.

drillQuestions.question: The risk involved in a Mars mission far _____ that of a Moon trip.

sentences.notes: "that"은 앞의 "scope and risk"를 대체하는 지시대명사이다. "far"는 비교급이나 동사를 강조하여 "훨씬"의 의미를 더한다.

koreanAlignment.en: involved in a crewed Earth-Mars mission
that of a trip to the Moon and back

sentences.original: A voyage to the Moon takes approximately three days with current technology, while crews would face two to three years without seeing other humans besides crewmates during a Mars mission.
sentences.translation: 달로의 항해는 현재 기술로 약 3일이 걸리는 반면, 화성 임무 동안 승무원들은 동료 승무원을 제외하고 2~3년 동안 다른 사람을 볼 수 없게 된다.

grammarElements.text: while crews would face two to three years without seeing other humans besides crewmates during a Mars mission
grammarElements.label: 대조 부사절

vocabulary.example: The voyage across the Atlantic took two weeks.
The trip takes approximately two hours.
The astronaut relied on her crewmates for support.

expressions.usage: 두 상황을 대조할 때
무언가 없이 지내는 상황을 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: 두 상황의 시간을 대비하는 패턴. while로 극적인 차이를 보여준다.
examples: A bus ride takes 30 minutes, while walking would take two hours.

drillQuestions.question: A Moon trip takes three days, _____ a Mars trip takes years.

sentences.notes: "3일 vs 2~3년"이라는 극적인 대비가 핵심이다. would face는 가정적/미래적 상황을 나타내며, 아직 화성 유인 임무가 실현되지 않았음을 보여준다.

sentences.original: The crew must endure no natural day and night cycle, a reduction in tasting ability, muscle wastage and bone-density loss over time.
sentences.translation: 승무원들은 자연적인 낮과 밤의 주기가 없고, 미각 능력의 감소, 근육 소모와 시간이 지남에 따른 골밀도 손실을 견뎌야 한다.

grammarElements.text: no natural day and night cycle
grammarElements.label: 목적어 1

vocabulary.example: Athletes must endure intense training.
There was a significant reduction in costs.
Muscle wastage occurs during prolonged inactivity.
Exercise helps maintain bone density.

expressions.usage: 점진적인 변화를 나타낼 때
피할 수 없는 고난을 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 여러 가지 어려움을 나열하며 견뎌야 함을 표현하는 패턴
examples: Hikers must endure extreme cold, thin air, and physical exhaustion.

drillQuestions.question: The crew must _____ many physical challenges.

sentences.notes: 우주 비행의 신체적 고난을 나열하는 문장. bone-density는 하이픈으로 연결된 복합명사로, 의학 용어이다. endure는 "참다, 견디다"라는 뜻의 중요한 동사이다.

koreanAlignment.en: no natural day and night cycle

sentences.original: Beyond Earth's atmosphere, astronauts on the International Space Station are exposed to 250 times the radiation experienced on Earth's surface.
sentences.translation: 지구 대기권 너머에서, 국제우주정거장의 우주비행사들은 지구 표면에서 경험하는 방사선의 250배에 노출된다.

grammarElements.text: astronauts on the International Space Station
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: 250 times the radiation experienced on Earth's surface
grammarElements.label: 전치사 목적어

vocabulary.example: Earth's atmosphere protects us from radiation.
Radiation from the sun can be harmful.
Workers were exposed to dangerous chemicals.

expressions.usage: 지구 범위를 벗어난 곳을 묘사할 때
배수를 구체적으로 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 특정 대상이 몇 배의 무언가에 노출된다는 패턴
examples: Workers in this factory are exposed to 10 times the noise level.
Pilots are exposed to twice the radiation of ground workers.

drillQuestions.question: Astronauts are exposed _____ 250 times the radiation.

sentences.notes: "250 times the radiation"은 배수 표현으로, "방사선의 250배"라는 뜻이다. exposed to는 수동태이며, "~에 노출되다"라는 의미의 중요한 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: astronauts on the International Space Station

sentences.original: This increases to 700 times the radiation for missions beyond Earth's orbit, without the protection of Earth's magnetic field.
sentences.translation: 이것은 지구 궤도 너머 임무에서는 지구 자기장의 보호 없이 방사선의 700배로 증가한다.

grammarElements.text: without the protection of Earth's magnetic field
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (결여)

vocabulary.example: The temperature increased by 5 degrees.
The satellite is in orbit around Earth.
Earth's magnetic field protects us from solar wind.
Sunscreen provides protection from UV rays.

expressions.usage: 수치가 특정 수준까지 올라감을 나타낼 때
보호 요소가 없는 상황을 설명할 때

patterns.pattern: This increases to [number] times for [condition], without [protection]
patterns.explanation: 조건이 바뀌면 수치가 더 증가한다는 것을 보여주는 패턴
examples: This increases to 10 times the normal level for mountain climbers, without the protection of lower elevation.

drillQuestions.question: Radiation increases _____ 700 times beyond Earth's orbit.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장의 250배에서 700배로 수치가 극적으로 증가하는 것을 보여준다. magnetic field(자기장)는 과학 기본 용어이다.

koreanAlignment.en: without the protection of Earth's magnetic field

sentences.original: Mars missions would combine long mission length with high radiation exposure.
sentences.translation: 화성 임무는 긴 임무 기간과 높은 방사선 노출을 결합하게 될 것이다.

vocabulary.example: The recipe combines sweet and sour flavors.
Prolonged sun exposure can cause skin damage.

expressions.usage: 두 가지를 합치는 것을 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 미래의 가정적 상황에서 A와 B가 결합된다는 표현
examples: The new job would combine travel with research.
The course would combine theory with practice.

drillQuestions.question: Mars missions would combine long duration _____ high radiation.

sentences.notes: would는 아직 실현되지 않은 미래의 상황을 나타낸다. combine A with B는 "A와 B를 결합하다"라는 뜻의 중요한 패턴이다.
pronunciationNotes: combine: /kəmˈbaɪn/ — "컴바인", 두 번째 음절에 강세. 명사일 때는 /ˈkɑːmbaɪn/

sentences.original: NASA and the ESA conducted studies following twins Scott and Mark Kelly.
sentences.translation: NASA와 ESA는 쌍둥이 스콧과 마크 켈리를 추적하는 연구를 수행했다.

grammarElements.text: following twins Scott and Mark Kelly
grammarElements.label: 현재분사 수식구 (대상)

vocabulary.example: The university conducted a survey of students.
A documentary following the lives of penguins.
The twins look exactly alike.

expressions.usage: 학술적/과학적 연구를 진행했음을 나타낼 때

patterns.explanation: 기관이 특정 대상을 추적하는 연구를 수행했다는 패턴
examples: The hospital conducted studies following patients with heart disease.
Researchers conducted studies following climate patterns.

drillQuestions.question: NASA _____ studies following the Kelly twins.

sentences.notes: NASA(미국 항공우주국)와 ESA(유럽 우주국)라는 두 기관이 주어로 쓰였다. following은 여기서 "~을 추적/관찰하는"이라는 현재분사적 의미이다.

koreanAlignment.en: following twins Scott and Mark Kelly

pronunciationNotes: ESA: /ˈiːsə/ — "이사"로 발음. European Space Agency의 약자

sentences.original: Scott conducted lengthy ISS missions while Mark remained on Earth.
sentences.translation: 스콧은 긴 ISS 임무를 수행한 반면, 마크는 지구에 남아 있었다.

vocabulary.example: The lengthy meeting lasted four hours.
She remained at home while others went out.

expressions.usage: 두 사람이나 사물의 대조적 상황을 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: A가 활동을 수행하는 동안 B는 다른 곳에 머물렀다는 대비 패턴
examples: The explorer conducted research in Antarctica while his family remained at home.

drillQuestions.question: Scott conducted ISS missions _____ Mark remained on Earth.

sentences.notes: 쌍둥이 연구의 핵심 설계를 보여주는 문장. "한 명은 우주에, 한 명은 지구에"라는 대비가 while로 효과적으로 표현된다.

sentences.original: Results showed Scott's immune system reacted to vaccines the same way as Mark's, meaning vaccines administered during long space missions should remain effective.
sentences.translation: 결과에 따르면 스콧의 면역 체계가 마크와 같은 방식으로 백신에 반응했으며, 이는 장기 우주 임무 동안 투여되는 백신이 여전히 효과적이어야 함을 의미한다.

grammarElements.text: Scott's immune system reacted to vaccines the same way as Mark's
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: meaning vaccines administered during long space missions should remain effective
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과/의미)

vocabulary.example: Exercise strengthens your immune system.
Scientists developed a new vaccine.
The nurse administered the medication.
The treatment was very effective.

expressions.usage: 두 가지를 비교하여 동일함을 나타낼 때
앞의 사실에서 도출되는 결론을 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: 연구 결과를 제시하고 분사구문으로 그 의미/결론을 덧붙이는 패턴
examples: Results showed increased heart rate, meaning the exercise was effective.

drillQuestions.question: Results showed the immune system reacted the same way, _____ vaccines remain effective.

sentences.notes: 쌍둥이 연구의 긍정적 결과를 보여준다. "meaning ~"은 분사구문으로, "which means ~"의 축약형이다. administer는 의학 용어로 "투여하다"라는 뜻이다.

koreanAlignment.en: vaccines administered during long space missions
koreanAlignment.ko: 장기 우주 임무 동안 투여되는 백신이

pronunciationNotes: vaccine: /vækˈsiːn/ — "백신", 두 번째 음절에 강세 (한국어 "백신"과 발음 차이 주의)

sentences.original: The research revealed differences in Scott's gene expression and changes in telomeres.
sentences.translation: 연구는 스콧의 유전자 발현에서의 차이와 텔로미어의 변화를 밝혀냈다.

vocabulary.example: The study revealed surprising results.
Gene expression determines how cells function.
Telomeres shorten with age.

expressions.usage: 연구 결과 발견된 차이를 보고할 때

patterns.explanation: 연구 결과를 두 가지로 제시하는 학술 보고 패턴
examples: The research revealed improved memory and increased focus.
The study revealed lower costs and higher efficiency.

drillQuestions.question: The research _____ differences in gene expression.

sentences.notes: telomere(텔로미어)는 전문 과학 용어이다. 다음 문장에서 설명해주므로, 모르는 용어가 나와도 뒤에 설명이 올 수 있다는 읽기 전략을 배울 수 있다.

sentences.original: Telomeres are the ends of DNA strands that protect chromosomes from damage.
sentences.translation: 텔로미어는 DNA 가닥의 끝부분으로, 염색체를 손상으로부터 보호한다.

vocabulary.example: A strand of hair was found at the scene.
Humans have 23 pairs of chromosomes.
The storm caused significant damage.

expressions.usage: 무언가가 해를 입지 않도록 지키는 기능을 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: 전문 용어를 정의하고 관계절로 기능을 설명하는 패턴
examples: Antibodies are proteins that fight infections.
Neurons are cells that transmit signals.

drillQuestions.question: Telomeres are the ends of DNA strands _____ protect chromosomes.

sentences.notes: 과학 용어를 독자에게 친절하게 설명하는 전형적인 방식이다. "A is B that C"의 구조로 정의 + 기능을 한 문장에 담는다.

koreanAlignment.en: are the ends of DNA strands

sentences.original: Scott's telomeres lengthened in space compared to Mark's.
sentences.translation: 스콧의 텔로미어는 마크의 것과 비교하여 우주에서 길어졌다.

vocabulary.example: The days lengthen in spring.

expressions.usage: 두 대상을 비교할 때

patterns.explanation: A의 것이 B의 것과 비교하여 변화했다는 비교 패턴
examples: The patient's health improved compared to the control group's.
The company's profits increased compared to last year's.

drillQuestions.question: Scott's telomeres lengthened _____ to Mark's.

sentences.notes: lengthen은 length(명사) + -en(접미사)으로 "길어지다"라는 동사가 된다. 영어에서 -en 접미사는 형용사/명사를 동사로 만든다(shorten, widen, strengthen 등).

sentences.original: While long telomeres were once thought to assist with longevity, extreme lengths increase cancer risk and other disorders.
sentences.translation: 긴 텔로미어가 한때 장수에 도움이 된다고 생각되었지만, 극단적인 길이는 암 위험과 다른 질환을 증가시킨다.

grammarElements.text: While long telomeres were once thought to assist with longevity
grammarElements.label: 양보 부사절

vocabulary.example: Good diet contributes to longevity.
Sleep disorders affect many people.
The desert has extreme temperatures.

expressions.usage: 과거에 믿어졌지만 현재는 다른 견해가 있을 때
무언가를 돕거나 지원하는 것을 나타낼 때

patterns.pattern: While [A] was once thought to [verb], [contrasting fact]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 믿음과 현재의 새로운 사실을 대비하는 패턴
examples: While butter was once thought to be unhealthy, moderate consumption is now considered fine.

drillQuestions.question: _____ long telomeres were once thought to help, extreme lengths increase cancer risk.

sentences.notes: "were once thought to"는 수동태 + 과거 시제로, 이전의 통설이 바뀌었음을 시사한다. While은 양보("~이지만")의 의미로 사용되었다.

sentences.original: To examine psychological challenges, researchers have conducted simulated Mars missions with analogue astronauts -- people role-playing astronauts without actually going to space.
sentences.translation: 심리적 도전을 조사하기 위해, 연구자들은 유사 우주비행사 — 실제로 우주에 가지 않고 우주비행사를 역할극하는 사람들 — 를 사용한 모의 화성 임무를 수행해 왔다.

grammarElements.text: people role-playing astronauts without actually going to space
grammarElements.label: 동격 설명구

vocabulary.example: The test measures psychological well-being.
Pilots train in simulated environments.
Analogue astronauts test equipment on Earth.

expressions.usage: 연구 목적을 밝힐 때
특정 역할을 연기하는 것을 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: 연구 목적과 방법을 한 문장에 제시하는 학술 패턴
examples: To understand memory loss, researchers have conducted brain scans.

drillQuestions.question: _____ examine psychological challenges, researchers conducted simulated missions.

sentences.notes: "analogue astronaut"는 실제 우주비행사가 아닌 모의 훈련 참가자를 뜻하는 전문 용어이다. 대시(--)로 이 용어를 부연 설명하는 방식이 효과적이다.
pronunciationNotes: psychological: /ˌsaɪkəˈlɑːdʒɪkl/ — "사이컬라지컬", 세 번째 음절에 강세. p는 묵음

sentences.original: Mars 500 was one prominent study conducted by the Russian Academy of Sciences' Institute of Biomedical Problems as a collaborative effort between Russian, European and Chinese space agencies.
sentences.translation: Mars 500은 러시아, 유럽, 중국 우주기관 간의 공동 노력으로 러시아 과학아카데미 생의학문제연구소가 수행한 하나의 저명한 연구였다.

grammarElements.text: conducted by the Russian Academy of Sciences' Institute of Biomedical Problems
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 수식구

grammarElements.text: as a collaborative effort between Russian, European and Chinese space agencies
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (방식)

vocabulary.example: She is a prominent scientist in her field.
The project was a collaborative effort between two universities.
Biomedical research has led to new treatments.

expressions.usage: 여러 기관이나 집단이 함께 협력하는 것을 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: 연구를 소개하면서 수행 기관과 성격을 설명하는 패턴
examples: The project was a major initiative conducted by the WHO as a global health effort.

drillQuestions.question: Mars 500 was one _____ study conducted by Russian scientists.

sentences.notes: 학술적 연구를 소개하는 전형적 구조이다. "collaborative effort"는 국제 협력을 강조하는 표현으로, 학술 논문이나 뉴스에서 자주 사용된다.

koreanAlignment.en: conducted by the Russian Academy of Sciences'
between Russian, European and Chinese space agencies

sentences.original: Over 17 months, six analogue astronauts lived in a simulated Mars mission environment.
sentences.translation: 17개월 동안 6명의 유사 우주비행사가 모의 화성 임무 환경에서 생활했다.

vocabulary.example: The experiment was conducted in a controlled environment.

expressions.usage: 특정 기간 동안 지속된 활동을 나타낼 때
특정 장소에서 거주하며 생활한 것을 나타낼 때

patterns.explanation: 특정 기간 동안 몇 명이 어떤 환경에서 생활했는지 설명하는 패턴
examples: Over 12 weeks, 20 volunteers lived in a remote cabin.
Over two years, four scientists lived in an underwater habitat.

drillQuestions.question: _____ 17 months, six astronauts lived in a simulated environment.

sentences.notes: "Over"는 여기서 "~에 걸쳐, ~동안"이라는 의미이다. "For 17 months"로도 바꿀 수 있지만, over가 더 서술적이다.

sentences.original: By including various nationalities, cultures and personalities, researchers could study team cohesion, conflict resolution, leadership, and problem-solving.
sentences.translation: 다양한 국적, 문화, 성격을 포함시킴으로써, 연구자들은 팀 결속력, 갈등 해결, 리더십, 문제 해결을 연구할 수 있었다.

grammarElements.text: By including various nationalities, cultures and personalities
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (수단/방법)

grammarElements.text: team cohesion, conflict resolution, leadership, and problem-solving
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (나열)

vocabulary.example: Team cohesion is essential for success.
Training in conflict resolution helps workplace relationships.
The team included members of five different nationalities.

expressions.usage: 방법이나 수단을 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: "~함으로써 ...할 수 있었다"는 수단-결과 패턴
examples: By combining different materials, engineers could create stronger buildings.

drillQuestions.question: _____ including various nationalities, researchers could study team dynamics.

sentences.notes: "By + 동명사"는 수단이나 방법을 나타내는 중요한 전치사 패턴이다. cohesion은 "결속력"이라는 뜻으로, 팀워크 관련 논의에서 핵심 단어이다.

sentences.original: However, maintaining synchronized sleeping patterns proved problematic.
sentences.translation: 그러나 동기화된 수면 패턴을 유지하는 것이 문제가 있는 것으로 드러났다.

vocabulary.example: It is important to maintain a healthy diet.
The swimmers performed synchronized routines.
The situation proved problematic for the team.

expressions.usage: 예상과 다르게 문제가 발생했음을 나타낼 때

patterns.explanation: 동명사 주어가 ~한 것으로 드러났다/판명되었다는 패턴. prove는 여기서 "판명되다"의 의미.
examples: Finding a solution proved difficult.
Running the experiment proved costly.

drillQuestions.question: Maintaining sleeping patterns _____ problematic.

sentences.notes: "proved problematic"에서 prove는 "판명되다"라는 연결동사(linking verb)로 사용되었다. 동명사구(Maintaining ...)가 주어 역할을 하는 문장 구조이다.

sentences.original: The first crewed Mars mission likely will not land on the surface initially.
sentences.translation: 최초의 유인 화성 임무는 처음에는 표면에 착륙하지 않을 가능성이 높다.

vocabulary.example: The game will likely be cancelled due to rain.
The plan initially seemed simple.
The plane landed safely at the airport.

expressions.usage: 부정적 예측을 조심스럽게 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 처음에는 ~하지 않을 것이라는 신중한 예측 패턴
examples: The new system likely will not work perfectly initially.
The technology likely will not be available initially.

drillQuestions.question: The Mars mission _____ will not land on the surface initially.

sentences.notes: likely는 형용사("가능성이 높은")와 부사("아마") 모두로 쓰인다. initially는 "처음에"라는 뜻으로, 나중에는 바뀔 수 있음을 시사한다.

sentences.original: Once advance supplies and tools reach a safe landing site, survival challenges begin.
sentences.translation: 사전 보급품과 도구가 안전한 착륙 지점에 도달하면, 생존의 도전이 시작된다.

grammarElements.text: Once advance supplies and tools reach a safe landing site
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절 (once = ~하면)

vocabulary.example: Advance tickets are cheaper.
Survival in the wilderness requires skill.

expressions.usage: 특정 조건이 충족된 후 다음 단계가 시작될 때

patterns.explanation: 조건이 충족되면 결과가 시작된다는 패턴. once = "일단 ~하면"
examples: Once the foundation is complete, construction begins.
Once the vaccine arrives, distribution begins.

drillQuestions.question: _____ supplies reach the landing site, survival challenges begin.

sentences.notes: "Once"는 "일단 ~하면"이라는 뜻으로, When보다 조건의 완료를 더 강조한다. advance는 형용사("사전의")로 supplies를 수식한다.

sentences.original: Basic requirements include breathable air, food, water, fuel and shelter.
sentences.translation: 기본 요구 사항에는 호흡 가능한 공기, 식량, 물, 연료, 거주지가 포함된다.

grammarElements.text: breathable air, food, water, fuel and shelter
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (나열)

vocabulary.example: Meeting the requirements is essential.
The air at high altitudes is less breathable.
They found shelter from the storm.
The rocket needs fuel to launch.

expressions.usage: 필수적으로 필요한 것들을 나열할 때

patterns.explanation: 기본적으로 필요한 것들을 나열하는 패턴
examples: Basic requirements include a passport, visa, and proof of vaccination.
Basic requirements include food, water, and electricity.

drillQuestions.question: Basic requirements _____ breathable air and water.

sentences.notes: 5가지 생존 필수 요소를 간결하게 나열한 문장이다. breathable은 breath(숨) + -able(가능한)의 합성으로, -able 접미사의 유용한 예시이다.
